title: Failed to pull the minikube base image v0.39
body:
### Issue Description

Seems like the `podman` fail to pull the `gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39`, I tried to pull the image with `docker pull` and it worked nicely...


```
[klesh@klesh-laptop] ~> minikube start                                                                                                                             1.287s
😄  minikube v1.30.1 on Ubuntu 22.04 (amd64)
    ▪ MINIKUBE_ROOTLESS=true
✨  Using the podman driver based on user configuration
📌  Using rootless Podman driver
👍  Starting control plane node minikube in cluster minikube
🚜  Pulling base image ...
💾  Downloading Kubernetes v1.26.3 preload ...
    > gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase...:  373.53 MiB / 373.53 MiB  100.00% 5.63 Mi
E0505 14:21:38.111533   21628 cache.go:188] Error downloading kic artifacts:  not yet implemented, see issue #8426
🔥  Creating podman container (CPUs=2, Memory=2200MB) ...
🤦  StartHost failed, but will try again: creating host: create: creating: setting up container node: preparing volume for minikube container: podman run --rm --name minikube-preload-sidecar --label created_by.minikube.sigs.k8s.io=true --label name.minikube.sigs.k8s.io=minikube --entrypoint /usr/bin/test -v minikube:/var gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39 -d /var/lib: exit status 125

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run ` podman pull gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39`
2. see error


### Describe the results you received

```
Trying to pull gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39...
WARN[0002] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: Get "https://storage.googleapis.com/artifacts.k8s-minikube.appspot.com/containers/images/sha256:67a4b1138d2d65c110e3ef7ba49c9e75d8d87f1150266297d90da979cfcc62e3": EOF
WARN[0003] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (2/3). Error: initializing source docker://gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39: Get "https://gcr.io/v2/token?scope=repository%3Ak8s-minikube%2Fkicbase%3Apull&service=gcr.io": EOF
WARN[0006] Failed, retrying in 1s ... (3/3). Error: initializing source docker://gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39: pinging container registry gcr.io: Get "https://gcr.io/v2/": EOF
Error: initializing source docker://gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39: pinging container registry gcr.io: Get "https://gcr.io/v2/": EOF
```

### Describe the results you expected

image to be pull successfully

API Version:  4.5.0

Additional environment details

### Additional information

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out @klesh!

I cannot reproduce in my environment.  The image pulls fine.  But the errors below indicate a transient network error (or on the registry side).  Are you running behind a proxy?  Can you reproduce it over a longer period of time?
```
time="2023-05-05T14:22:02+08:00" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: copying system image from manifest list: reading blob sha256:4befd8315658446f4458b5ce76c91f000cb0e569bbeb0ef641620a0024dcf52c: Get \"https://gcr.io/v2/k8s-minikube/kicbase/blobs/sha256:4befd8315658446f4458b5ce76c91f000cb0e569bbeb0ef641620a0024dcf52c\": EOF"
time="2023-05-05T14:22:07+08:00" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (2/3). Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: Get \"https://storage.googleapis.com/artifacts.k8s-minikube.appspot.com/containers/images/sha256:67a4b1138d2d65c110e3ef7ba49c9e75d8d87f1150266297d90da979cfcc62e3\": EOF"
time="2023-05-05T14:22:08+08:00" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (3/3). Error: initializing source docker://gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39: Get \"https://gcr.io/v2/token?scope=repository%3Ak8s-minikube%2Fkicbase%3Apull&service=gcr.io\": EOF"
Error: initializing source docker://gcr.io/k8s-minikube/kicbase:v0.0.39: pinging container registry gcr.io: Get "https://gcr.io/v2/": EOF
```

> produce in my environment. The image pulls fine. But the errors below indicate a transient network error (or on the registry side). Are you running behind a proxy? Can you reproduce it over a longer period of time?

Thanks for the quick response.
Yes, I'm behind a proxy and I just realize that `podman` and `docker` were using different proxies...

@vrothberg Thank you so much for taking the time to help. It is tough to deal with ALL THESE NON-SENSE PROXY MESS!🥲
No worries at all, @klesh.  I appreciate you having reached out and am glad you got it working.
